status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 4 / COMBINATION ANALYTICS BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Fixed-ratio Modified GRF (1-29) analogue and ipamorelin supplier blend

components.name: Modified GRF (1-29) / supplier-labelled CJC-1295 without DAC
components.identity_note: Must not be treated as CJC-1295 with DAC

mechanism_summary: Combined GHRH-receptor and ghrelin-receptor stimulation may alter GH/IGF-1 exposure, but interaction, compatibility and clinical benefit cannot be inferred from the components.
evidence_maturity: No direct fixed-ratio combination-specific human evidence has been closed. Marketplace CJC-1295-without-DAC nomenclature requires identity control.
regulatory_headline: UNAPPROVED SUPPLIER GH-AXIS COMBINATION / CJC-1295 WITH DAC EVIDENCE DOES NOT TRANSFER

catalogue_supplier_skus.specification: CJC-1295 without DAC 5 mg + Ipamorelin 5 mg; 10 mg x 10 vials
CJC-1295 without DAC 10 mg + Ipamorelin 10 mg; 20 mg x 10 vials

closure_class: DUAL GH-AXIS BLEND / CJC-NAMING AND STRUCTURAL IDENTITY
closure_state: BLOCKED — MODIFIED GRF SEQUENCE, IPAMORELIN FORM, FIXED RATIO, COMPATIBILITY, PK/PD AND DIRECT HUMAN COMBINATION EVIDENCE UNVERIFIED
authoritative_product_boundary: The catalogue phrase CJC-1295 without DAC is controlled as a marketplace name for Modified GRF (1-29), not CJC-1295 with DAC. Primary literature establishes separate GRF(1-29) analogue and ipamorelin pharmacology, but not the CP10 or CP20 co-lyophilised products.
critical_closure_controls: CP10 and CP20 remain strength variants of one blend family
Separate GRF and ipamorelin evidence does not validate the blend
GH/IGF-1, glucose, neoplasm and fluid-retention risks remain active
No GH, dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
